abstract: The ad placements for a campaign.
overview:
## Discussion

Use the following combination of campaign and ad group values for `supplySources`.

For payload examples, see [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/AdChannelType], [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/BillingEventType], [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/PricingModel], and [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Campaign] object. For more information about campaigns and ad groups, see [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Create-a-Campaign] and [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Create-an-Ad-Group].

### Today tab compatibility

Campaigns with an `APPSTORE_TODAY_TAB` `SupplySource` reach users when they come to the App Store to discover apps. For an ad to display in the Today tab, your custom product page metadata — *app name* and *subtitle* — need to be localized in the `defaultLanguage` of all countries or regions where your ad is serving. For example, ads serving in the United States and Mexico require a product page with localized `en-US` and a product page with `es-MX` assets. See [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Get-Supported-Countries-or-Regions].

iPhone is the only supported [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/DeviceClass] that you can use with a Today tab `SupplySource`. Today tab ad groups not targeting iPhone are placed on hold with the following [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/AdGroupServingStateReasons]: `TARGETED_DEVICE_CLASS_NOT_SUPPORTED_SUPPLY_SOURCE`.

As part of review:

Use [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Find-Ad-Creative-Rejection-Reasons] or [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Gets-a-product-page-reason] to look up rejection reasons. See [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/ProductPageReason] for rejection reason enumerations.

# SupplySource

**Type**

The ad placements for a campaign.

## Declaration

```swift
string SupplySource
```

## Discussion

Use the following combination of campaign and ad group values for `supplySources`.



For payload examples, see [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/AdChannelType], [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/BillingEventType], [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/PricingModel], and [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Campaign] object. For more information about campaigns and ad groups, see [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Create-a-Campaign] and [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Create-an-Ad-Group].

### Today tab compatibility

Campaigns with an `APPSTORE_TODAY_TAB` `SupplySource` reach users when they come to the App Store to discover apps. For an ad to display in the Today tab, your custom product page metadata — *app name* and *subtitle* — need to be localized in the `defaultLanguage` of all countries or regions where your ad is serving. For example, ads serving in the United States and Mexico require a product page with localized `en-US` and a product page with `es-MX` assets. See [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Get-Supported-Countries-or-Regions].

iPhone is the only supported [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/DeviceClass] that you can use with a Today tab `SupplySource`. Today tab ad groups not targeting iPhone are placed on hold with the following [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/AdGroupServingStateReasons]: `TARGETED_DEVICE_CLASS_NOT_SUPPORTED_SUPPLY_SOURCE`.

As part of review:

- Ads that are pending review are on hold until Apple completes the review.
- Ads that are incompatible with Today tab guidelines don’t receive approval.
- The system doesn’t serve rejected ads.

Use [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Find-Ad-Creative-Rejection-Reasons] or [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/Gets-a-product-page-reason] to look up rejection reasons. See [doc://com.apple.appleads/documentation/apple_ads/ProductPageReason] for rejection reason enumerations.

## Data Types

- **AdChannelType**: The channel type of an ad in a campaign.
- **BillingEventType**: The type of billing event for a campaign.
- **CampaignCountryOrRegionsServingStateReasons**: Reasons that displays when a campaign can’t run.
- **CampaignDisplayStatus**: The status of the campaign.
- **CampaignServingStateReasons**: Reasons the system provides when a campaign can’t run.
- **CampaignServingStatus**: The status of the campaign.
- **CampaignStatus**: The status of the campaign.
- **PaymentModel**: The payment model that you set.
